config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: SAYA TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap menjadi batas utama agar kepentingan masyarakat tidak berubah menjadi alasan untuk menekan kebebasan, martabat, dan perlindungan orang per orang. Dalam praktiknya, kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai lewat pembatasan yang benar-benar perlu dan adil, bukan dengan mengorbankan hak dasar seenaknya. Saya lebih percaya pada keseimbangan yang tegas: masyarakat kuat justru kalau individu dilindungi, bukan dikalahkan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should automatically take priority over individual rights, because in the United States that kind of logic has too often been used to excuse abuse, discrimination, and government overreach. At the same time, individual rights are not absolute in every situation, since public safety and basic order sometimes require limits, like laws against harm or fraud. But the burden should be on society to justify any restriction, not on the individual to prove why their rights matter. If we reverse that standard, we make it too easy to sacrifice liberty in the name of the common good.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus diutamakan di atas hak individu secara umum. Dari pengalaman saya melihat konteks Indonesia, dalih “demi kepentingan bersama” terlalu mudah dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan berlebihan, dari kebebasan berpendapat sampai perlakuan tidak adil terhadap kelompok kecil. Hak individu memang tidak absolut, tetapi justru masyarakat yang sehat adalah masyarakat yang membatasi negara dan mayoritas agar tidak semena-mena atas nama ketertiban. Jadi, prioritasnya harus kebalik: hak individu jadi dasar, lalu kepentingan sosial dibenarkan hanya kalau benar-benar perlu, proporsional, dan bisa dipertanggungjawabkan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: The statement goes too far for me, because in the United States I have seen how “society first” can become a blank check for censorship, discrimination, and government abuse. That said, I do not think individual rights should be treated as unlimited; rules against violence, fraud, and threats are necessary because a society cannot function if everyone’s liberty is totally unchecked. What matters is the standard: society should have to prove a restriction is necessary, narrow, and fair, rather than assuming the individual must yield by default. If we put society’s interests above rights in principle, we make it much easier to silence unpopular people whenever the majority feels justified.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu karena di Indonesia dalih “demi masyarakat” terlalu sering dipakai untuk menekan kelompok kecil dan membenarkan keputusan yang tidak adil. Bagi saya, hak individu harus jadi dasar utama, sementara kepentingan sosial hanya boleh membatasi hak kalau benar-benar perlu, sempit cakupannya, dan bisa diuji secara adil. Memang masyarakat tidak bisa berjalan kalau semua orang bebas tanpa batas, tetapi batas itu harus ditujukan untuk mencegah kerugian nyata, bukan untuk memuaskan mayoritas atau penguasa. Kalau prioritas dibalik, yang sering terjadi bukan ketertiban, melainkan penyalahgunaan kuasa atas nama kebaikan bersama.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe the interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual as a general rule. From a United States perspective, that idea has too often been used to justify censorship, discrimination, and government overreach, especially when the majority decides that unpopular people are expendable. I do think rights can be limited for real public safety reasons, but only when the restriction is necessary, narrow, and clearly justified. If society gets priority by default, then individual liberty becomes conditional on whatever the powerful call the common good.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
